expectations.evidence: All 5 hex codes present: #000814, #1E2333, #E6E2DE, #F9F6F0, #FAFBFA — listed in a full palette table
Both #E0B848 (Gold) and #1894C9 (Blue) explicitly present with usage guidance
Poppins Bold, Montserrat, and Open Sans all named in the typography table
Poppins Bold to H1/H2; Montserrat to subheadings; Open Sans to body — explicit table
Arial Bold, Helvetica, and Georgia fallbacks listed in the typography table
Includes a Quick Copy-Paste Reference block and a verbatim AI prompt block — both directly usable
Uses fabricated hex codes (#0D1B2A, etc.) — none of the 5 official main colors are present
No mention of #E0B848 or #1894C9 — uses Electric Blue and Cyan instead
Recommends Inter and Space Grotesk — none of the three brand fonts appear
No mapping to Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans roles — wrong fonts throughout
No fallback fonts mentioned — no awareness of the brand font stack
Includes a Quick-Reference Card with organized blocks — format is usable even though content is wrong
All 7 colors defined as RGBColor() calls with correct positional RGB arguments
DEEP_NAVY = RGBColor(0, 8, 20) — matches #000814 exactly
GOLD = RGBColor(224, 184, 72) — matches #E0B848 exactly
BLUE = RGBColor(24, 148, 201) — matches #1894C9 exactly
run.font.name set to Poppins with run.font.bold = True in heading code block
run.font.name set to Montserrat for subheading and listed in font table
run.font.name set to Open Sans for body text and listed in font table
DEEP_NAVY, DARK_NAVY, OFF_WHITE, NEAR_WHITE, WHITE, GOLD, BLUE — all 7 defined
Uses RGBColor() calls with hex notation — syntax valid even though values are wrong
Uses RGBColor(0x0D, 0x0D, 0x0D) = (13,13,13) instead of (0, 8, 20) — fabricated value
No Gold color — uses SECONDARY_ACCENT purple instead
PRIMARY_ACCENT = (0,122,255) — wrong, should be (24,148,201)
Assigns Montserrat Bold to headings — no mention of Poppins anywhere
Montserrat incorrectly used for headings, not subheadings — role mapping wrong
Assigns Inter (not Open Sans) to body text — no mention of Open Sans
Only 6 colors defined — no Near White or Gold as separate entries
Section 1 explicitly recommends Deep Navy #000814 as primary, Dark Navy #1E2333 as option
Section 2 table: dark bg to White #FAFBFA or Off-White #E6E2DE; light bg to Deep Navy #000814

Multiple mentions: premium, dark, corporate-tech; premium, high-authority
Recommends deep navy or charcoal (#1A2B4A range) — correct direction but wrong hex codes
Mentions dark bg to light text logic, but without specific Bishop AI hex values
No Gold mentioned — recommends Electric Blue and Cyan as accents instead
Mentions Montserrat and Inter but never Poppins — no authoritative 3-tier hierarchy
6 numbered styling sections — format actionable even without correct brand values
Mentions authority and intelligence, technical sophistication, trustworthiness

notes: actionable_for_design_tool passes in both configurations — non-discriminating; good structure is a baseline Claude capability regardless of brand knowledge
without_skill pass rate has high variance (0.32 +/- 0.28): LinkedIn eval partially passes because generic design advice directionally aligns, but specific brand values are entirely fabricated
with_skill is 100% pass rate across all 3 evals — the skill is the sole source of truth for exact hex codes, RGB values, and font names; without it the model fabricates plausible-but-wrong values
time cost is negligible: skill adds essentially 0s on average (the skill file read is offset by the model being more efficient with clear guidance)
tokens slightly higher with skill (+1694) due to reading SKILL.md — a worthwhile tradeoff for brand accuracy
